title: Structure-aware substrate merge/oid driver: 3-way merge over items, edges, config, and objects keyed off the content-parent DAG, ending in invariant re-validation and multi-parent lineage
context: FGAP-004: pi-context records per-item lineage (content_parent DAG) + a content-addressed objects/ store but has no operation that merges two divergent substrate states. This is the substrate-level reconvergence framework on which the convergent-sequence field (FGAP-005) and branch-pinned substrates depend; it is the reconvergence counterpart to clone/fork divergence (DEC-0002).
decision: 1) Merge-base from the content-parent DAG: use content_parent lineage to find the lowest common ancestor version per item (and for the substrate), giving the base for every 3-way comparison. 2) Per-item 3-way merge over B/X/Y: added-on-one -> include; removed-on-one and untouched-on-other -> remove; modified-on-one -> take it; modified-on-both -> field-level merge; removed-on-one and modified-on-other -> conflict. 3) Field-kind-dispatched field merge: scalar/object fields take the side that changed vs base (both-identical -> take; both-different -> declared deterministic policy or conflict); SEQUENCE fields delegate to the convergent ordered-sequence field-type (FGAP-005/DEC-0005) — this driver is the framework that field-kind plugs into. 4) Edge-set merge: edges are (parent, child, relation_type) triples reconciled by 3-way set ops against base; after item deletes, re-check edges whose endpoints no longer exist and drop or conflict. 5) Object-store merge by blob union (equal hash == equal content -> conflict-free). 6) Config/schema 3-way merge; divergent redefinition of the same block_kind / relation_type / schema is a surfaced conflict, not an auto-merge. 7) Multi-parent lineage: merged items get a new content_hash with content_parent referencing BOTH source versions — requires content_parent to admit >=2 parents (a node, not a single link). 8) Post-merge invariant re-validation: run the full declared-invariant + lens-validator pass on the merged state (branch-valid states can merge to globally invalid, e.g. a requires-edge whose target was deleted on the other branch); failures surface as conflicts. 9) Structured conflict set: typed records {item_id, field, base, ours, theirs} (+ edge/schema analogues), not inline text markers, so the auto-merged majority lands cleanly alongside the unresolved remainder. 10) Cherry-pick as a sub-case: applying a single item-version delta reuses the same base-diff-and-replay machinery scoped to one item/change-set.
consequences: content_parent must become multi-parent (a DAG node admitting >=2 parents), shared with the clone/fork lineage model (DEC-0002).
The merge driver + merge-base finder live in the block-api; output is deterministic regardless of which side is named 'ours'.
The convergent ordered-sequence field-type (FGAP-005) is one field-kind plugin into step 3's field-merge dispatch; this driver is the substrate-level framework.
Cherry-pick is delivered by the same machinery, no separate mechanism.
Foundational and currently absent — branch-pinned substrates and the convergent-sequence field both depend on it.
